ground_truth.title: Top 50 Biggest Gaming Companies in 2025 by Revenue
ground_truth.author: Andrea Knezovic
ground_truth.main_content: The gaming industry is booming. It's projected to reach $363.2 billion in revenue by 2027 (Statista). Wondering which gaming companies are driving this growth despite industry layoffs? Check out this list of the top 50 biggest gaming companies in 2025 by yearly revenue. Sony Interactive Entertainment (SIE) is the video game division of Sony Group Corporation, responsible for the PlayStation brand and related products. As of September 2024, the PlayStation 5 has shipped over 65 million units worldwide. Tencent Games, a subsidiary of Tencent Holdings, remains one of the world's biggest gaming companies in terms of revenue. In Q3 2024, Tencent reported a 47% year-over-year net profit increase. Microsoft Gaming (also known as Xbox Game Studios) is the video game division of Microsoft that oversees development and publishing of games. The $68.7 billion acquisition of Activision Blizzard added franchises like Call of Duty and World of Warcraft to Microsoft's portfolio. Nintendo is a multinational consumer electronics and video game company known for its revolutionary contributions to the gaming industry, creating beloved franchises like Mario, Zelda, and Pokémon. NetEase Games is a leading Chinese internet technology company providing online services centered on content, community, communications, and commerce. Electronic Arts (EA) is a prominent American video game company known for developing and publishing popular game franchises under the EA Sports label. Epic Games is an American video game and software developer known for creating Fortnite, which has generated over $12 billion in lifetime revenue since its launch in 2017. Take-Two Interactive is a major American video game holding company known for its publishing labels Rockstar Games and 2K, renowned for critically acclaimed games with high-quality storytelling. Embracer Group is a Swedish video game holding company that has rapidly expanded through acquisitions, owning over 130 game development studios and more than 900 franchises. miHoYo is a Chinese video game developer known for creating visually stunning and story-rich games like Genshin Impact, which has generated over $4 billion in global revenue. The article continues with detailed rankings for companies 11-50 including Roblox Corporation, Playtika, Ubisoft, Nexon, Square Enix, Bandai Namco Entertainment, Sega, Netmarble, NCSoft, Konami Digital Entertainment, Krafton, Perfect World, Garena, Wizards of the Coast, Capcom, Niantic, CD Projekt Red, and many others, each with their revenue figures, headquarters locations, and popular game franchises.
